Interactive Astronomy Platform — by Biswajit Jana @2026

AstroBis

Built by Biswajit Jana - a browser-native space lab with a realistic Solar System atlas, live ISS mission view, JPL NEO watch, exoplanet archive, and 3D stellar atlas.

Open Solar System Atlas Browse Exoplanets
Scroll to explore

✨

The Universe by the Numbers

The cosmos is vast beyond imagination. Here's a glimpse.

🌍
0+
Exoplanets Confirmed
Worlds orbiting other stars — and counting
🌌
0B
Galaxies Observable
In the observable universe alone
⏳
0M
Years — Universe Age
Years since the Big Bang
☀️
0
Planets in our System
Each unique — from tiny Mercury to vast Jupiter

What You Can Explore

From the Oort Cloud to live orbital traffic, AstroBis keeps the useful science close to the surface.

🌌

Solar System Atlas

Three.js + WebGL

A compressed real-distance Solar System with planets, dwarf planets, moon counts, asteroid belt, Kuiper belt, heliopause, and Oort Cloud regions.

Open Atlas →
🔭

Exoplanet Explorer

NASA Archive

Query NASA Exoplanet Archive data with measured radius, mass, orbit, temperature, discovery method, procedural portraits, and source references.

Browse Planets →
🛰️

ISS Mission Control

Live Orbit

Track the station over a textured 3D Earth with live telemetry, orbit projection, cloud layer, and ground-track map.

Track ISS →
☄️

NEO Watch

JPL CAD

Look ahead through 2050 using JPL close-approach data with distance filters, speed, size estimates, and risk-proxy sorting.

Open Watch →
⭐

3D Stellar Atlas

Star Data

Navigate bright stars using real RA/Dec coordinates, spectral colors, constellation guides, distance shells, and an H-R diagram.

Open Atlas →
📸

NASA APOD

Daily NASA

Each day, NASA selects an astronomy image with scientific context. AstroBis keeps it alongside the live data tools.

See Today's Image →

Today's View from NASA

The Astronomy Picture of the Day — curated by NASA scientists.

🔭
Loading today's cosmos…

Start with the Solar System

Open the new AstroBis atlas, travel from Mercury to the Oort Cloud, then jump into exoplanets, live ISS telemetry, future asteroid approaches, and the stellar map.

Open the Solar System Atlas
AstroBis
